title: abelicea hirta
intro: Abelicea hirta, also known as the sticky mouse-ear or hairy abelia, is a shrub native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Abelicea hirta can be prepared in various ways, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
